title: Full grounded-vs-blank-slate eval (accuracy/groundedness/fit; zero invented numbers) - full version of eval-009
context: Benefits-Navigator is an openly-licensed corpus of plain-language, primary-source-cited, multilingual, accessible guides to public-assistance programs (SNAP, Medicaid/CHIP, WIC, TANF, LIHEAP, SSI, housing, EITC, Lifeline/ACP), delivered as a static site, printable PDFs, and structured data with an optional retrieval-grounded explainer. It is a HIGH risk-tier project: it provides information, not legal/benefits advice, never makes an individualized eligibility determination, and always routes people to the official application and free human help. No partner, credentialed reviewer, or requestor is yet secured. This task is the full grounded-vs-blank-slate eval (the complete version of eval-009): an LLM judge scores accuracy, groundedness/citation, and fit, and asserts zero invented eligibility numbers. Grounded+cited must clearly beat blank-slate or the explainer is withheld.
objective: Implement the full eval running the explainer grounded+cited vs. blank-slate on fixtures, with an LLM judge scoring accuracy, groundedness/citation, and fit, and a hard zero-invented-numbers assertion.
acceptanceCriteria: Runs the explainer (if built) grounded+cited vs. blank-slate on fixtures; LLM judge scores accuracy, groundedness/citation, and fit; asserts zero invented eligibility numbers; reports the delta
Grounded+cited must clearly beat blank-slate or the explainer is withheld
Results recorded with a go/no-go decision for the explainer that the M3/M4 milestones honor
